name: Maison Hale
aesthetic: restrained, tonal interiors where materials age honestly
brief: For Maison Hale, the strategy is subtraction. The professional field is loud with revival and spectacle this cycle — steer the brand toward the quiet, material-led signals (plaster, patinated metal, natural stone) and let client demand for natural-stone baths and quiet luxury validate the restraint. Lead with permanence, not novelty.

name: Atelier Verde
aesthetic: organic modern rooms that bring the garden indoors
brief: Atelier Verde sits at the intersection of two rising currents: professional interest in companion planting and botanical craft, and surging client demand for indoor-garden and wellness rooms. The strategy writes itself — own the biophilic narrative and pair it with the practical demand signals (light wood, natural stone, glass-enclosed showers) clients are already searching for.

name: Saint & Roe
aesthetic: layered, colour-saturated rooms rich in antiques and craft
brief: Saint & Roe should lean into maximalism while it has cultural permission. Murano glass, botanical ceramics and ornamental craft are rising on the professional side, and client demand for ornate millwork and botanical wallpaper gives the look commercial cover. The brand's edge is conviction — go saturated where competitors hedge.

name: Kestrel Studio
aesthetic: monolithic, architectural interiors in steel, stone and glass
brief: Kestrel's positioning is reinforced by the hard data: steel architectural elements and curved forms are among the strongest client-demand signals this cycle, and the 1990s and architectural revival on the professional side give the language a fashion tailwind. Strategy: double down on structure and material clarity; ignore the soft, decorative trends entirely.

name: Marlow House
aesthetic: culturally-rooted rooms built around global artisanal craft
brief: Marlow House is built for this moment of global, craft-forward design. Professional signals around Indonesian modernism, African-diaspora references and artisanal ceramics align tightly with the studio's story; client demand for vintage influence and natural materials gives it reach. Strategy: foreground the makers and the provenance in every piece of brand communication.
